course.title: Containers for GenAI & Agentic AI — The Open-Source Way
course.tagline: Build one realistic system, one step per module — from a bare model call to a shipped multi-agent platform.
course.description: A hands-on 2-day corporate workshop / self-paced course (School of DevOps & AI MLOps/LLMOps catalogue) that teaches container-native GenAI and agentic AI end to end: serving local models, packaging them as OCI artifacts, naive RAG, agentic RAG with MCP tools, multi-agent orchestration, and supply-chain security — all validated live on a 16 GB laptop with open-source tooling.
course.author: Gourav Shah
course.audience: Platform, DevOps, and MLOps engineers comfortable with container basics (build/run/volumes/networks, Compose) who want to run and ship GenAI/agentic workloads container-native.
course.project_spine: Two connected use cases built step by step: the Acme Docs Assistant (naive RAG, Day 1 payoff) and the Acme Support Agent → Incident Crew (agentic RAG → multi-agent, Day 2), shipped together as the Acme AI Support Platform in the capstone.
prereq_knowledge: Container basics: build, run, volumes, networks, Compose file format
Git and GitHub; basic CI/CD concepts
Command-line fluency on macOS or Windows
Basic Python helps; no ML background required

stack.container_runtime: Rancher Desktop (docker + docker compose); OCI/Compose-Spec standard, also runs on Colima/OrbStack/Podman/Docker Desktop
stack.models: Ollama (native host) — qwen2.5:1.5b, qwen2.5:3b, nomic-embed-text; CPU-vLLM — SmolLM2

install.linux: Install Docker Engine or Rancher Desktop

lab.environment_noun: Rancher Desktop lab environment (containers) + native Ollama model server on the host
lab.setup: Rancher Desktop (docker + compose) running locally; Ollama installed natively on the host serving qwen2.5:1.5b (and nomic-embed-text for RAG modules) at :11434 — on Apple Silicon the model server always runs native, never containerized, and containers reach it at http://host.docker.internal:11434. Each module ships its own labs/<id>/compose.yaml (spine mode: per-module, not grow-in-place).

modules.label: M1 · Container-Native GenAI
modules.title: Container-Native GenAI
objectives: Explain why container-native (not Docker-native) is the right framing for GenAI workloads
Identify what containers buy an AI stack
Understand the Apple Silicon GPU reality and why the model server runs native
Map the 2026 landscape of declarative agents vs orchestration frameworks
Use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint as the universal contract
modules.lab_intent: Stand up the runtime and prove the container-to-native-host wiring: a containerized caller reaches the native Ollama model server via host.docker.internal.

modules.label: M2 · Serving Local Models
modules.title: Serving Local Models
objectives: Compare open serving engines: Docker Model Runner, Ollama, llama.cpp, LocalAI
Explain the OpenAI-compatible endpoint as a universal contract across engines
Choose GGUF models sized for a 16 GB laptop
Wire an app to a model endpoint using two patterns (native host vs containerized)
modules.lab_intent: Call the OpenAI-compatible endpoint from a containerized app pointed at the native Ollama server, and swap engines without changing the app.

modules.label: M3 · Production Serving with vLLM
modules.title: Production Serving with vLLM
objectives: Explain why vLLM exists: throughput and batching versus one-request-at-a-time serving
Keep the same OpenAI-compatible contract on a bigger engine
Run the CPU vLLM track locally; understand the GPU track for throughput (documented, not run here)
Apply quantization in practice and recognize common operational gotchas
modules.lab_intent: Serve SmolLM2 on CPU vLLM in a container and exercise it through the same OpenAI-compatible contract used in M2.

modules.label: M3B · Customizing Models with LoRA/QLoRA (optional, GPU-gated)
modules.title: Customizing Models with LoRA/QLoRA
objectives: Decide when to fine-tune versus when not to
Explain LoRA as targeted, low-rank adaptation (the 'sticky notes on a textbook' analogy)
Understand the fine-tuning toolchain and the GPU reality for this workload
Treat the frozen container as the reproducible unit of the experiment
modules.lab_intent: Run a LoRA/QLoRA fine-tune following the Apple Silicon and NVIDIA GPU tracks, and produce a versioned adapter artifact.

modules.label: M4 · Packaging Models as OCI Artifacts
modules.title: Packaging Models as OCI Artifacts
objectives: Explain the problem with loose model files and why OCI artifacts fit models well
Use KitOps and ModelKit (CNCF + ORAS) to package a model
Author a Kitfile as a shipping manifest
Trace the full flow from Kitfile to registry to serving node, including selective pull and multi-registry portability
modules.lab_intent: Pack a model into a ModelKit with KitOps, push it to a registry, and selectively pull just the layers a serving node needs.

modules.label: M5 · Docs Assistant — Naive RAG
modules.title: Docs Assistant — Naive RAG
objectives: Explain the ungrounded-answer problem naive RAG solves
Map the anatomy of a GenAI application end to end
Build the naive-RAG pipeline: ingest, embed, retrieve, generate
Use ChromaDB as a lightweight vector store and recognize where naive RAG breaks down
modules.lab_intent: Build the Acme Docs Assistant: ingest runbooks, embed them into ChromaDB, and serve grounded answers through a containerized naive-RAG pipeline.

modules.label: M6 · Declarative Agent — Agentic RAG
modules.title: Declarative Agent — Agentic RAG
objectives: Evolve a docs assistant into an agent
Define an agent declaratively with the three files that define Aria (AGENTS.md + Agent Skills)
Contrast agentic RAG with naive RAG
Wire MCP tools via ToolHive, and apply guardrails and memory
modules.lab_intent: Turn the Docs Assistant into the Aria support agent: declare its profile, connect MCP tools through ToolHive, and add guardrails and memory.

modules.label: M7 · Multi-Agent Incident Crew
modules.title: Multi-Agent Incident Crew
objectives: Explain why multi-agent systems earn their keep (the hospital, not superhero, analogy)
Design a pipeline of specialized agents (Triage → Investigate → Fix → Review)
Compare two paths to multi-agent orchestration: CrewAI and LangGraph
Run one shared model endpoint behind four agents and use a Reviewer as a human-in-the-loop proxy
modules.lab_intent: Stand up the Incident Crew with CrewAI (and optionally LangGraph), all agents sharing one native model endpoint, with the Reviewer agent gating output.

modules.label: M8 · Securing & Governing AI Workloads
modules.title: Securing & Governing AI Workloads
objectives: Map the AI supply-chain pipeline end to end
Generate an SBOM as the ingredients label for an image
Scan for vulnerabilities with two independent scanners and reconcile disagreement
Sign images for tamper-evidence and sandbox agent/tool/generated-code execution
Harden the container image
modules.lab_intent: Generate an SBOM with syft, scan with trivy and grype, sign the image with cosign, and sandbox agent tool execution.

modules.label: Capstone · Ship the Acme AI Platform
modules.title: Capstone: Ship the Acme AI Support Platform
objectives: Assemble the Docs Assistant, Support Agent, and Incident Crew into one deployable Acme AI Support Platform
Keep the model native and every other component containerized, per the course's defining architectural rule
Prove the platform ships unchanged across any OCI runtime via CI
modules.lab_intent: Wire the consolidated compose.yaml for the full platform, run it end to end, and ship it through CI to prove OCI-runtime portability.
